3:T48a,High-quality multilingual training data is essential for effectively pretraining large language models (LLMs). Yet, the availability of suitable open-source multilingual datasets remains limited. Existing state-of-the-art datasets mostly rely on heuristic filtering methods, restricting both their cross-lingual transferability and scalability. Here, we introduce JQL, a systematic approach that efficiently curates diverse and high-quality multilingual data at scale while significantly reducing computational demands. JQL distills LLMs' annotation capabilities into lightweight annotators based on pretrained multilingual embeddings. These models exhibit robust multilingual and cross-lingual performance, even for languages and scripts unseen during training. Evaluated empirically across 35 languages, the resulting annotation pipeline substantially outperforms current heuristic filtering methods like Fineweb2. JQL notably enhances downstream model training quality and increases data retention rates. Our research provides practical insights and valuable resources for multilingual data curation, raising the standards of multilingual dataset development.4:T70f,Recent advancements in speech-language models have yielded significant improvements in speech tokenization and synthesis. However, effectively mapping the complex, multidimensional attributes of speech into discrete tokens remains challenging. This process demands acoustic, semantic, and contextual information for precise speech representations. Existing speech representations generally fall into two categories: acoustic tokens from audio codecs and semantic tokens from speech self-supervised learning models. Although recent efforts have unified acoustic and semantic tokens for improved performance, they overlook the crucial role of contextual representation in comprehensive speech modeling. Our empirical investigations reveal that the absence of contextual representations results in elevated Word Error Rate (WER) and Word Information Lost (WIL) scores in speech transcriptions. To address these limitations, we propose two novel distillation approaches: (1) a language model (LM)-guided distillation method that incorporates contextual information, and (2) a combined LM and self-supervised speech model (SM)-guided distillation technique that effectively distills multimodal representations (acoustic, semantic, and contextual) into a comprehensive speech tokenizer, termed DM-Codec. The DM-Codec architecture adopts a streamlined encoder-decoder framework with a Residual Vector Quantizer (RVQ) and incorporates the LM and SM during the training process. Experiments show DM-Codec significantly outperforms state-of-the-art speech tokenization models, reducing WER by up to 13.46%, WIL by 9.82%, and improving speech quality by 5.84% and intelligibility by 1.85% on the LibriSpeech benchmark dataset. Code, samples, and checkpoints are available at https://github.com/mubtasimahasan/DM-Codec.5:T40d,Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and, at its core, reward modeling have become a crucial part of training powerful large language models (LLMs). One commonly overlooked factor in training high-quality reward models (RMs) is the effect of the base model, which is becoming more challenging to choose given the rapidly growing pool of LLMs. In this work, we present a systematic analysis of the effect of base model selection on reward modeling performance. Our results show that the performance can be improved by up to 14% compared to the most common (i.e., default) choice. Moreover, we showcase the strong statistical relation between some existing benchmarks and downstream performances. We also demonstrate that the results from a small set of benchmarks could be combined to boost the model selection ($+$18% on average in the top 5-10). Lastly, we illustrate the impact of different post-training steps on the final performance and explore using estimated data distributions to reduce performance prediction error.6:T5f9,Large language models (LLMs) exhibit remarkable similarity to neural activity in the human language network. However, the key properties of language shaping brain-like representations, and their evolution during training as a function of different tasks remain unclear. We here benchmark 34 training checkpoints spanning 300B tokens across 8 different model sizes to analyze how brain alignment relates to linguistic competence. Specifically, we find that brain alignment tracks the development of formal linguistic competence -- i.e., knowledge of linguistic rules -- more closely than functional linguistic competence. While functional competence, which involves world knowledge and reasoning, continues to develop throughout training, its relationship with brain alignment is weaker, suggesting that the human language network primarily encodes formal linguistic structure rather than broader cognitive functions. We further show that model size is not a reliable predictor of brain alignment when controlling for feature size and find that the correlation between next-word prediction, behavioral alignment and brain alignment fades once models surpass human language proficiency. Finally, using the largest set of rigorous neural language benchmarks to date, we show that language brain alignment benchmarks remain unsaturated, highlighting opportunities for improving future models. Taken together, our findings suggest that the human language network is best modeled by formal, rather than functional, aspects of language.7:T4c5,To assist users in complex tasks, LLMs generate plans: step-by-step instructions towards a goal. While alignment methods aim to ensure LLM plans are helpful, they train (RLHF) or evaluate (ChatbotArena) on what users prefer, assuming this reflects what helps them. We test this with Planorama: an interface where 126 users answer 300 multi-step questions with LLM plans. We get 4388 plan executions and 5584 comparisons to measure plan helpfulness (QA success) and user preferences on plans, and recreate the setup in agents and reward models to see if they simulate or prefer what helps users. We expose: 1) user/model preferences and agent success do not accurately predict which plans help users, so common alignment feedback can misalign with helpfulness; 2) this gap is not due to user-specific preferences, as users are similarly successful when using plans they prefer/disprefer; 3) surface-level cues like brevity and question similarity strongly link to preferences, but such biases fail to predict helpfulness. In all, we argue aligning helpful LLMs needs feedback from real user interactions, not just preferences of what looks helpful, so we discuss the plan NLP researchers can execute to solve this problem.8:T501,Despite the remarkable capabilities of large language models, current training paradigms inadvertently foster \textit{sycophancy}, i.e., the tendency of a model to agree with or reinforce user-provided information even when it's factually incorrect. To address this challenge, we introduce \textbf{SMART} (Sycophancy Mitigation through Adaptive Reasoning Trajectories), which reframes sycophancy as a \textit{reasoning optimization problem} rather than an output alignment issue. SMART is a two-stage framework comprising: (1) Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Monte Carlo Tree Search (UA-MCTS), which dynamically adjusts model exploration based on state-level uncertainty to collect high-quality, diverse reasoning trajectories alongside both stepwise progress and final outcome rewards; and (2) progress-based reinforcement learning, which fine-tunes the model using the collected trajectories and reward signals to reinforce effective reasoning patterns. Through extensive experiments, we show that SMART significantly reduces sycophantic behavior while preserving strong performance on out-of-distribution inputs and maintaining general capabilities. These results underscore the importance of optimizing internal reasoning mechanisms to build more truthful and aligned AI assistants.9:T506,The ability of large language models (LLMs) to validate their output and identify potential errors is crucial for ensuring robustness and reliability. However, current research indicates that LLMs struggle with self-correction, encountering significant challenges in detecting errors. While studies have explored methods to enhance self-correction in LLMs, relatively little attention has been given to understanding the models'internal mechanisms underlying error detection. In this paper, we present a mechanistic analysis of error detection in LLMs, focusing on simple arithmetic problems. Through circuit analysis, we identify the computational subgraphs responsible for detecting arithmetic errors across four smaller-sized LLMs. Our findings reveal that all models heavily rely on $\textit{consistency heads}$--attention heads that assess surface-level alignment of numerical values in arithmetic solutions. Moreover, we observe that the models'internal arithmetic computation primarily occurs in higher layers, whereas validation takes place in middle layers, before the final arithmetic results are fully encoded. This structural dissociation between arithmetic computation and validation seems to explain why smaller-sized LLMs struggle to detect even simple arithmetic errors.a:T439,With new large language models (LLMs) emerging frequently, it is important to consider the potential value of model-agnostic approaches that can provide interpretability across a variety of architectures. While recent advances in LLM interpretability show promise, many rely on complex, model-specific methods with high computational costs. To address these limitations, we propose NormXLogit, a novel technique for assessing the significance of individual input tokens. This method operates based on the input and output representations associated with each token. First, we demonstrate that during the pre-training of LLMs, the norms of word embeddings effectively capture token importance. Second, we reveal a significant relationship between a token's importance and the extent to which its representation can resemble the model's final prediction. Extensive analyses reveal that our approach outperforms existing gradient-based methods in terms of faithfulness and offers competitive performance in layer-wise explanations compared to leading architecture-specific techniques.b:T434,One of the challenges of quantizing a large language model (LLM) is the presence of outliers. Outliers often make uniform quantization schemes less effective, particularly in extreme cases such as 4-bit quantization. We introduce KurTail, a new post-training quantization (PTQ) scheme that leverages Kurtosis-based rotation to mitigate outliers in the activations of LLMs. Our method optimizes Kurtosis as a measure of tailedness. This approach enables the quantization of weights, activations, and the KV cache in 4 bits. We utilize layer-wise optimization, ensuring memory efficiency. KurTail outperforms existing quantization methods, offering a 13.3\% boost in MMLU accuracy and a 15.5\% drop in Wiki perplexity compared to QuaRot. It also outperforms SpinQuant with a 2.6\% MMLU gain and reduces perplexity by 2.9\%, all while reducing the training cost. For comparison, learning the rotation using SpinQuant for Llama3-70B requires at least four NVIDIA H100 80GB GPUs, whereas our method requires only a single GPU, making it a more accessible solution for consumer GPU.c:T4fe,Understanding when and how linguistic knowledge emerges during language model training remains a central challenge for interpretability. Most existing tools are post hoc, rely on scalar metrics, or require nontrivial integration effort, making comprehensive interpretability analysis difficult to deploy and maintain. We introduce TRACE, a modular toolkit for training and inference-time interpretability analysis of transformer models. It enables lightweight, in-training analysis of linguistic and representational signals, including features probing, intrinsic dimensionality, Hessian curvature, and output diagnostics. It integrates with ABSynth, a controllable synthetic corpus generator that provides structured annotations for precise evaluation of linguistic feature acquisition. Experiments with autoregressive transformers demonstrate that TRACE reveals developmental phenomena such as early syntactic emergence, delayed semantic acquisition, and representational compression, signals overlooked by traditional scalar metrics such as loss or accuracy. With minimal integration effort, the tool enables layer-wise diagnostics, convergence-based early stopping, and detection of structural errors, making transformer analysis interpretable, actionable, and reproducible.d:T527,Instruction-following made modern large language models (LLMs) helpful assistants. However, the key to taming LLMs on complex instructions remains mysterious, for that there are huge gaps between models trained by open-source community and those trained by leading companies. To bridge the gap, we propose a simple and scalable approach UltraIF for building LLMs that can follow complex instructions with open-source data. UltraIF first decomposes real-world user prompts into simpler queries, constraints, and corresponding evaluation questions for the constraints. Then, we train an UltraComposer to compose constraint-associated prompts with evaluation questions. This prompt composer allows us to synthesize complicated instructions as well as filter responses with evaluation questions. In our experiment, for the first time, we successfully align LLaMA-3.1-8B-Base to catch up with its instruct version on 5 instruction-following benchmarks without any benchmark information, using only 8B model as response generator and evaluator. The aligned model also achieved competitive scores on other benchmarks. Moreover, we also show that UltraIF could further improve LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct through self-alignment, motivating broader use cases for the method. Our code is available at https://github.com/kkk-an/UltraIF.e:T449,Context faithfulness is essential for reliable reasoning in context-dependent scenarios. However, large language models often struggle to ground their outputs in the provided context, resulting in irrelevant responses. Inspired by the emergent expert specialization observed in mixture-of-experts architectures, this work investigates whether certain experts exhibit specialization in context utilization, offering a potential pathway toward targeted optimization for improved context faithfulness. To explore this, we propose Router Lens, a method that accurately identifies context-faithful experts. Our analysis reveals that these experts progressively amplify attention to relevant contextual information, thereby enhancing context grounding. Building on this insight, we introduce Context-faithful Expert Fine-Tuning (CEFT), a lightweight optimization approach that selectively fine-tunes context-faithful experts. Experiments across a wide range of benchmarks and models demonstrate that CEFT matches or surpasses the performance of full fine-tuning while being significantly more efficient.f:T40b,Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) have proven to be powerful tools for interpreting neural networks by decomposing hidden representations into disentangled, interpretable features via sparsity constraints. However, conventional SAEs are constrained by the fixed sparsity level chosen during training; meeting different sparsity requirements therefore demands separate models and increases the computational footprint during both training and evaluation. We introduce a novel training objective, \emph{HierarchicalTopK}, which trains a single SAE to optimise reconstructions across multiple sparsity levels simultaneously. Experiments with Gemma-2 2B demonstrate that our approach achieves Pareto-optimal trade-offs between sparsity and explained variance, outperforming traditional SAEs trained at individual sparsity levels. Further analysis shows that HierarchicalTopK preserves high interpretability scores even at higher sparsity. The proposed objective thus closes an important gap between flexibility and interpretability in SAE design.10:T4ad,Short video platforms are evolving rapidly, making the identification of inappropriate content increasingly critical. Existing approaches typically train separate and small classification models for each type of issue, which requires extensive human-labeled data and lacks cross-issue generalization. We propose a reasoning-enhanced multimodal large language model (MLLM) pretraining paradigm for unified inappropriate content detection. To address the distribution gap between short video content and the original pretraining data of MLLMs, as well as the complex issue definitions, we introduce three targeted pretraining tasks: (1) \textit{Caption}, to enhance the MLLM's perception of video details; (2) \textit{Visual Question Answering (VQA)}, to deepen the MLLM's understanding of issue definitions and annotation guidelines; (3) \textit{Chain-of-Thought (CoT)}, to enhance the MLLM's reasoning capability. Experimental results show that our pretraining approach significantly improves the MLLM's performance in both zero-shot and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) settings. In addition, our pretrained model demonstrates strong generalization capabilities to emergent, previously unseen issues.11:T44d,Large language models (LLMs) remain unreliable for global enterprise applications due to substantial performance gaps between high-resource and mid/low-resource languages, driven by English-centric pretraining and internal reasoning biases. This inconsistency undermines customer experience and operational reliability in multilingual settings such as customer support, content moderation, and information retrieval. Even with advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, we observe up to an 29% accuracy drop in non-English languages compared to English. We propose a practical, batch-wise alignment strategy for fine-tuning LLMs, leveraging semantically equivalent multilingual data in each training batch to directly align model outputs across languages. This approach improves non-English accuracy by up to 23.9% without compromising English performance, model reasoning, or retrieval quality. Our method is simple to implement, scalable, and integrates seamlessly with existing LLM training&deployment pipelines, enabling more robust and equitable multilingual AI solutions in industry.12:T4a8,Cross-lingual consistency should be considered to assess cross-lingual transferability, maintain the factuality of the model knowledge across languages, and preserve the parity of language model performance. We are thus interested in analyzing, evaluating, and interpreting cross-lingual consistency for factual knowledge. To facilitate our study, we examine multiple pretrained models and tuned models with code-mixed coreferential statements that convey identical knowledge across languages. Interpretability approaches are leveraged to analyze the behavior of a model in cross-lingual contexts, showing different levels of consistency in multilingual models, subject to language families, linguistic factors, scripts, and a bottleneck in cross-lingual consistency on a particular layer. Code-switching training and cross-lingual word alignment objectives show the most promising results, emphasizing the worthiness of cross-lingual alignment supervision and code-switching strategies for both multilingual performance and cross-lingual consistency enhancement. In addition, experimental results suggest promising result for calibrating consistency in the test time via activation patching.13:T5d8,Showing incorrect answers to Large Language Models (LLMs) is a popular strategy to improve their performance in reasoning-intensive tasks. It is widely assumed that, in order to be helpful, the incorrect answers must be accompanied by comprehensive rationales, explicitly detailing where the mistakes are and how to correct them. However, in this work we present a counterintuitive finding: we observe that LLMs perform better in math reasoning tasks when these rationales are eliminated from the context and models are left to infer on their own what makes an incorrect answer flawed. This approach also substantially outperforms chain-of-thought prompting in our evaluations. These results are consistent across LLMs of different sizes and varying reasoning abilities. To gain an understanding of why LLMs learn from mistakes more effectively without explicit corrective rationales, we perform a thorough analysis, investigating changes in context length and answer diversity between different prompting strategies, and their effect on performance. We also examine evidence of overfitting to the in-context rationales when these are provided, and study the extent to which LLMs are able to autonomously infer high-quality corrective rationales given only incorrect answers as input. We find evidence that, while incorrect answers are more beneficial for LLM learning than additional diverse correct answers, explicit corrective rationales over-constrain the model, thus limiting those benefits.14:T58e,Large language models (LLMs) struggle with compositional generalisation, limiting their ability to systematically combine learned components to interpret novel inputs. While architectural modifications, fine-tuning, and data augmentation improve compositionality, they often have limited adaptability, face scalability constraints, or yield diminishing returns on real data. To address this, we propose CARMA, an intervention that enhances the stability and robustness of compositional reasoning in LLMs while preserving fine-tuned performance. CARMA employs mutual information regularisation and layer-wise stability constraints to mitigate feature fragmentation, ensuring structured representations persist across and within layers. We evaluate CARMA on inverse dictionary modelling and sentiment classification, measuring its impact on semantic consistency, performance stability, and robustness to lexical perturbations. Results show that CARMA reduces the variability introduced by fine-tuning, stabilises token representations, and improves compositional reasoning. While its effectiveness varies across architectures, CARMA's key strength lies in reinforcing learned structures rather than introducing new capabilities, making it a scalable auxiliary method. These findings suggest that integrating CARMA with fine-tuning can improve compositional generalisation while maintaining task-specific performance in LLMs.15:T5a3,Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse domains, but developing high-performing models for specialized applications often requires substantial human annotation -- a process that is time-consuming, labor-intensive, and expensive. In this paper, we address the label-efficient learning problem for supervised finetuning (SFT) by leveraging task-diversity as a fundamental principle for effective data selection. This is markedly different from existing methods based on the prompt-diversity. Our approach is based on two key observations: 1) task labels for different prompts are often readily available; 2) pre-trained models have significantly varying levels of confidence across tasks. We combine these facts to devise a simple yet effective sampling strategy: we select examples across tasks using an inverse confidence weighting strategy. This produces models comparable to or better than those trained with more complex sampling procedures, while being significantly easier to implement and less computationally intensive. Notably, our experimental results demonstrate that this method can achieve better accuracy than training on the complete dataset (a 4\% increase in MMLU score). Across various annotation budgets and two instruction finetuning datasets, our algorithm consistently performs at or above the level of the best existing methods, while reducing annotation costs by up to 80\%.16:T4f9,Through a controlled study, we identify a systematic deficiency in the multimodal grounding of Vision Language Models (VLMs). While VLMs can recall factual associations when provided a textual reference to an entity; their ability to do so is significantly diminished when the reference is visual instead. Forcing VLMs to rely on image representations of an entity halves their ability to recall factual knowledge, suggesting that VLMs struggle to link their internal knowledge of an entity with its image representation. We show that such linking failures are correlated with the expression of distinct patterns in model internal states, and that probes on these internal states achieve over 92% accuracy at flagging cases where the VLM response is unreliable. These probes can be applied, without retraining, to identify when a VLM will fail to correctly answer a question that requires an understanding of multimodal input. When used to facilitate selective prediction on a visual question answering task, the probes increase coverage by 7.87% (absolute) while also reducing the risk of error by 0.9% (absolute). Addressing the systematic, detectable deficiency is an important avenue in language grounding, and we provide informed recommendations for future directions.17:T4d3,Despite significant advancements in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs), a gap remains, particularly regarding their interpretability and how they locate and interpret textual information within images. In this paper, we explore various LVLMs to identify the specific heads responsible for recognizing text from images, which we term the Optical Character Recognition Head (OCR Head). Our findings regarding these heads are as follows: (1) Less Sparse: Unlike previous retrieval heads, a large number of heads are activated to extract textual information from images. (2) Qualitatively Distinct: OCR heads possess properties that differ significantly from general retrieval heads, exhibiting low similarity in their characteristics. (3) Statically Activated: The frequency of activation for these heads closely aligns with their OCR scores. We validate our findings in downstream tasks by applying Chain-of-Thought (CoT) to both OCR and conventional retrieval heads and by masking these heads. We also demonstrate that redistributing sink-token values within the OCR heads improves performance. These insights provide a deeper understanding of the internal mechanisms LVLMs employ in processing embedded textual information in images.18:T58c,Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning enhances performance of large language models, but questions remain about whether these reasoning traces faithfully reflect the internal processes of the model. We present the first comprehensive study of CoT faithfulness in large vision-language models (LVLMs), investigating how both text-based and previously unexplored image-based biases affect reasoning and bias articulation. Our work introduces a novel, fine-grained evaluation pipeline for categorizing bias articulation patterns, enabling significantly more precise analysis of CoT reasoning than previous methods. This framework reveals critical distinctions in how models process and respond to different types of biases, providing new insights into LVLM CoT faithfulness. Our findings reveal that subtle image-based biases are rarely articulated compared to explicit text-based ones, even in models specialized for reasoning. Additionally, many models exhibit a previously unidentified phenomenon we term ``inconsistent''reasoning - correctly reasoning before abruptly changing answers, serving as a potential canary for detecting biased reasoning from unfaithful CoTs. We then apply the same evaluation pipeline to revisit CoT faithfulness in LLMs across various levels of implicit cues. Our findings reveal that current language-only reasoning models continue to struggle with articulating cues that are not overtly stated.19:T5b8,Language models built from various sources are the foundation of today's NLP progress. However, for many low-resource languages, the diversity of domains is often limited, more biased to a religious domain, which impacts their performance when evaluated on distant and rapidly evolving domains such as social media. Domain adaptive pre-training (DAPT) and task-adaptive pre-training (TAPT) are popular techniques to reduce this bias through continual pre-training for BERT-based models, but they have not been explored for African multilingual encoders. In this paper, we explore DAPT and TAPT continual pre-training approaches for African languages social media domain. We introduce AfriSocial, a large-scale social media and news domain corpus for continual pre-training on several African languages. Leveraging AfriSocial, we show that DAPT consistently improves performance (from 1% to 30% F1 score) on three subjective tasks: sentiment analysis, multi-label emotion, and hate speech classification, covering 19 languages. Similarly, leveraging TAPT on the data from one task enhances performance on other related tasks. For example, training with unlabeled sentiment data (source) for a fine-grained emotion classification task (target) improves the baseline results by an F1 score ranging from 0.55% to 15.11%. Combining these two methods (i.e. DAPT + TAPT) further improves the overall performance. The data and model resources are available at HuggingFace.1a:T505,In real world translation scenarios, terminology is rarely one-to-one. Instead, multiple valid translations may appear in a terminology dictionary, but correctness of a translation depends on corporate style guides and context. This can be challenging for neural machine translation (NMT) systems. Luckily, in a corporate context, many examples of human post-edits of valid but incorrect terminology exist. The goal of this work is to learn how to disambiguate our terminology based on these corrections. Our approach is based on preference optimization, using the term post-edit as the knowledge to be preferred. While previous work had to rely on unambiguous translation dictionaries to set hard constraints during decoding, or to add soft constraints in the input, our framework requires neither one-to-one dictionaries nor human intervention at decoding time. We report results on English-German post-edited data and find that the optimal combination of supervised fine-tuning and preference optimization, with both term-specific and full sequence objectives, yields statistically significant improvements in term accuracy over a strong NMT baseline without significant losses in COMET score. Additionally, we release test sets from our post-edited data and terminology dictionary.0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"mx-auto max-w-5xl px-4 与真实数据训练、简单数据扩充、或不同 synthetic source 的比较；有价值的 ablation 会包括去重、温度采样、混合顺序和 curriculum。具体数字现在无法确认，但若论文做得好，最有价值的产出会是一组可操作的“使用边界”，而不是一句笼统的 synthetic data 有用/没用。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper tackles a pressing pretraining question: when does synthetic data help LLM pretraining, how does the benefit scale, and where do the failure modes appear? Current discussion is polarized—synthetic data is viewed either as a practical substitute for scarce high-quality text or as a route to distribution drift and model collapse. The paper aims to turn that debate into a controlled scaling study.","importance":"This is the most directly pretraining-relevant paper in the list and potentially one of the most impactful. The contribution is both data-level and systems-level: it is not just asking whether synthetic data helps, but under what ratios, qualities, and scales it helps or hurts. If the study is rigorous, its conclusions would directly inform mixture design, continued pretraining, self-bootstrapping pipelines, and data strategy in low-resource settings.","technical_approach":"The paper appears to be a systematic empirical study rather than a new synthetic-data generator. That is exactly the right contribution for the field right now because practitioners mostly lack reliable laws: what quality threshold synthetic text needs to be useful, whether it substitutes for real data or complements it, and why too much of it can damage generalization. The likely design varies real/synthetic mixture ratios, source model quality, filtering and deduplication, token budgets, and model size, then measures both optimization behavior and downstream utility. mixing 的差异在于更细粒度，能避免整个数据桶被粗暴同权。这样设计有效的原因是：质量主要影响梯度信噪比，多样性主要影响覆盖与泛化，按样本联合建模比按来源标签近似更准确；代价是打分成本更高，且质量—多样性代理一旦失真会引入系统性偏差。","experiments":"没有摘要，无法确认模型大小、训练 token 数、比较的 mixing baseline 和具体增益。关键实验应报告在相同 的收益是持续存在还是只在小模型/小数据阶段明显。值得关注的消融是质量分数来源、多样性度量方式、以及联合权重函数对结果的敏感性。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a central pretraining issue: data mixing should not optimize quality and diversity in isolation, because over-optimizing either one can hurt the final model. Many existing recipes operate at the corpus level with fixed mixture weights, which is too coarse when quality and coverage vary substantially within each source.","importance":"This is directly relevant to pretraining practice. Its value is in shifting the mixing problem from corpus-level budgeting to sample-level allocation, which could improve token efficiency, preserve long-tail coverage, and change how we reason about data scaling.","technical_approach":"SampleMix appears to be a sample-wise mixing strategy that jointly accounts for data quality and diversity during pretraining. The likely mechanism is to score each sample along those two axes and convert the scores into sampling probabilities or a curriculum schedule. Compared with source-level mixture weighting, this is more fine-grained and can avoid overcommitting to a dataset bucket whose average quality hides substantial internal variance. Lion-style attempts without v trade off stability. The paper asks whether m alone, used as its own normalizer, can replace v entirely.","importance":"A systems-level pretraining contribution. Dropping v halves optimizer state — a big deal for memory and bandwidth at scale. If AdamS matches AdamW's convergence and stability, it is a drop-in improvement for base-model training. These contributions matter not by pushing quality but by reducing cost at equal loss.","technical_approach":"AdamS keeps the EMA momentum m and uses a norm / second-order statistic of m itself as the update normalizer (replacing 1/√v̂ in AdamW), leaving only one optimizer state. The intuition: v estimates gradient variance, but in large-batch LLM training gradients and momentum are highly correlated, so m already carries magnitude information and v is redundant. Design choice lies in the aggregation granularity (per-param / block / layer) to retain Adam's per-coordinate adaptivity.","experiments":"Compared against AdamW, Lion, and other low-state optimizers on both LLM pretraining and posttraining. contribution. Existing PRMs frequently collapse all mistakes into a binary or scalar signal, which makes the reward too coarse to guide long-chain reasoning effectively.","importance":"This is a method contribution with strong relevance to the pretrain-posttrain loop. This matters because group-based RL methods are often seen as stable but conservative, especially for reasoning tasks that need exploration beyond the model’s current modes.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with direct relevance to RLHF and reasoning optimization. Provides an automated, dynamic influence-based solution for pretrain data mixing, challenging the static mixing paradigm of the Chinchilla era.","technical_approach":"Proposes Domain Impact-aware Data Sampling (DIDS). It dynamically computes the gradient similarity or loss impact of different domains on the model's current state. If domain A effectively reduces the loss of domain B (positive transfer), A's sampling weight is dynamically increased when optimizing for B, and vice versa, forming an adaptive curriculum.","experiments":"Validated on standard pretraining corpora. LLM scaling law 多是正向拟合，描述 loss 如何随参数、数据、算力变化，但难以从目标性能反推最优资源配置。工业上真正常见的问题是 inverse problem：给定 compute 或目标 loss，该怎么反算模型、数据和训练步数，而传统经验公式往往不稳定或只在局部区间有效。","importance":"这是偏方法论和系统决策层的工作。对 pretrain 的价值很直接：如果能把 scaling law 从事后拟合变成可反求、可规划的工具，那么它会影响预算分配、数据采购、token 配比以及 mid-train/cooldown 策略，是训练前决策层面的高杠杆研究。","technical_approach":"核心方法应是把 scaling law 建模为一个可求逆的问题，而不是只做单向回归。它可能引入显式逆映射、约束优化或基于观测点的逆问题求解框架，用已有训练曲线或离散实验点反推出最优配置。和 Chinchilla 这类经验律相比，区别不在于承认缩放规律存在，而在于强调可识别性和反演稳定性：参数之间常常共线，直接反算很容易病态，所以论文大概率会加入正则、先验或结构约束。方法是否成立，取决于它是否比简单网格搜索或经验配方更稳、更可迁移。","experiments":"当前没有摘要，因此无法确认论文使用的是自有训练 runs 还是公开文献数据，也无法确认是否覆盖不同模型家族。理想的实验应比较 forward-fit 再反求 与 原生 inverse formulation 的误差差异，报告在预算受限场景下的配置命中率，并检验外推到新规模时是否稳定。若只在窄范围内拟合得好，这类方法的实用性会很有限；但具体结果目前无法确认。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper focuses on an operational gap in scaling-law research: most laws are fit in the forward direction, but practitioners often need the inverse mapping—what model size, data size, and training budget should be chosen to hit a target loss or compute budget. Standard empirical laws are not always stable enough for that inversion.","importance":"This is a methodology and planning contribution with direct relevance to pretraining. If scaling laws can be framed as reliable inverse problems, they become useful before training starts, not just as post-hoc descriptions. That would affect compute allocation, data acquisition, and training schedule design.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is to reformulate scaling-law estimation as an inverse problem with explicit constraints or regularization, instead of fitting a forward curve and inverting it naively. That matters because inverse mappings are often ill-conditioned: different combinations of parameters and data can produce similar losses. A good method therefore needs identifiability and robustness, not just a lower regression error.","experiments":"No abstract or numbers are available, so there is no basis for reporting exact model scales, datasets, or gains. The pretraining relevance is strong: multi-token prediction could become part of the modeling recipe for discrete speech generation, not just a serving trick, if it improves latency without materially degrading naturalness.","technical_approach":"VocalNet appears to train a speech LLM to predict multiple future tokens per decoding step. The idea is conceptually simple but nontrivial in speech: local temporal redundancy makes short-horizon multi-token prediction plausible, but aggressive horizon expansion can easily break prosody and consistency. So the value of the method depends on how it controls compounding errors while still reducing the number of decoding steps.","experiments":"No abstract or metrics are provided, so the exact tokenization setup, model sizes, speedups, and quality numbers cannot be summarized reliably. 方法。关键在于‘at scale’：很多小规模实验中有效的选择策略，在大 token 预算下可能被随机覆盖度抵消；作者应当通过控制训练预算、数据池质量和模型大小，去分离算法增益与规模效应。这个设计的价值在于它检验了数据选择方法是否真的提供超越随机基线的净收益，而不是只在小样本 regime 看起来有优势。","experiments":"没有摘要，无法确认模型参数、token need’具体差多少。最关键的结果应是：在多个预算点和多个模型尺度上，随机选择与质量过滤、相似度采样、主动选择、课程学习等方法的性能差距；如果标题成立，差距应当很小，或者只在低预算/低质量池中明显。最有价值的分析会是边界条件：什么时候随机真够用，什么时候复杂选择仍值得做，比如极噪声数据池、特定领域迁移或稀缺高价值数据场景。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The Practitioners want to extrapolate final loss from an early fraction of the run. The paper models the time dimension explicitly.","importance":"Directly valuable for pretraining engineering. Existing laws tell you how to spend compute but not 'given I've burned 20% of steps, where will I land'. A stable temporal scaling law enables early kill of bad recipes, informed LR scheduling, and data-mix decisions — a natural extension of Chinchilla into training dynamics.","technical_approach":"Fit a low-parameter functional form for loss(step) (likely power-law + offset, e.g. a + b·t^(-c)) and show the form is invariant across N, D, batch size, with only the coefficients varying. The core claim is that fitting the first k% of the curve lets you extrapolate final loss; then the temporal coefficients themselves re-map onto N and D, nesting neatly under Chinchilla.","experiments":"Validated across multiple LLM pretraining runs spanning model and data scales. Key result expected: final-loss prediction error within a few percent from the first k% of steps, and recovery of Chinchilla-style dependence after regressing the temporal coefficients onto (N, D). Li"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ZhangZSWKLSLL25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ZhangZSWKLSLL25","_ee":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-emnlp.429/","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":8,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"KV cache 压缩中，token 数量和量化精度之间存在最优权衡点，但此前工作要么只做 token 剪枝、要么只做 KV 量化，缺乏对两者联合优化的系统分析。在固定显存预算下，应该保留更多低精度 token 还是更少高精度 token？","importance":"方法级贡献。对 KV cache 压缩给出了一个实用的设计原则：在大多数场景下，保留更多 token + 更低精度优于保留更少 token + 更高精度。这对长上下文推理的工程实践有直接指导意义，也为 pretrain 阶段考虑推理效率提供了参考。","technical_approach":"核心思路是将 token 剪枝和 KV 量化统一到同一个显存预算约束下进行联合搜索。作者系统地扫描了不同 token 保留比例与量化位宽的组合，分析在固定 memory budget 下哪种配置在下游任务上表现最好。关键发现是：token 数量对性能的边际贡献通常大于精度的边际贡献，因此最优策略偏向 more tokens + lower precision。","experiments":"基于多个 LLM（具体模型和参数量需看原文）在长上下文任务上进行评测。主要 baseline 包括纯 token 剪枝方法（如 H2O、StreamingLLM 等）和纯 KV 量化方法。实验覆盖不同压缩比，核心结论是在相同显存预算下，联合策略（更多 token + 更低精度）一致优于单一维度压缩。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"KV cache compression faces a fundamental trade-off between keeping more tokens at lower precision vs. fewer tokens at higher precision. Prior work treats token eviction and KV quantization separately, lacking a unified analysis under a fixed memory budget.","importance":"Provides a practical design principle for KV cache compression: more tokens at lower precision generally outperforms fewer tokens at higher precision. This is a method-level contribution with direct implications for long-context serving and inference-aware pretraining.","technical_approach":"Jointly searches over token retention ratio and quantization bit-width under a fixed memory budget. The key finding is that the marginal value of retaining additional tokens typically exceeds the marginal value of higher precision, leading to an optimal operating point biased toward more tokens + lower bits.","experiments":"Evaluated across multiple LLMs on long-context benchmarks. Baselines include token-only methods (H2O, StreamingLLM, etc.) and quantization-only methods. Prior unified token approaches improved over pure codecs or pure SSL semantics, but often assumed that downstream language models could recover the missing context. This work claims that assumption is too optimistic.","importance":"This is a meaningful pretraining-side contribution for speech language modeling. The main value is not a new modeling paradigm but a sharper definition of what a useful speech tokenizer should preserve: acoustic detail, semantic content, and contextual structure. That matters because once the discrete interface loses context, later LM stages cannot fully recover it.","technical_approach":"DM-Codec is a distillation-based speech tokenizer that explicitly brings multimodal representations into the tokenization process, with contextual representation added alongside acoustic and semantic signals. The key design claim is that context is not a luxury feature; it carries dependencies that directly affect perplexity and generation quality. Compared with prior unified speech tokenizers, the difference is the emphasis on preserving context at the tokenization stage rather than leaving it entirely to the LM. The likely trade-off is higher tokenizer complexity and a tighter bitrate–information balance.","experiments":"The strongest experimental statement in the abstract is mechanistic rather than numeric: removing contextual representations leads to higher perplexity and worse speech generation. So the direction is clear, but the strength of the gain cannot be calibrated from the abstract alone. The most important details in the full paper are how much perplexity drops and whether the benefits hold under low-bitrate settings.","authors_note":"The author list includes Aman Chadha. In practice this is often treated as a default decision, while most attention goes to preference data and RM training details. With many candidate LLMs now available, that shortcut is no longer safe.","importance":"This is a high-value systems analysis for the post-training stack, with clear implications for the pretrain–posttrain interface. The broader lesson is that a model that is strong at generation is not automatically the best substrate for reward modeling.","technical_approach":"The contribution is a systematic empirical study rather than a new reward model architecture. 为什么会与人脑语言网络的神经活动相似，这种 brain alignment 在训练过程中到底跟哪类能力一起长。过去很多工作报告过 LLM 与脑信号的相关性，但很少系统拆开‘形式语言能力’和‘功能性语言能力’各自的作用，也缺少沿训练过程的动态视角。","importance":"这是一个机制分析方向的重要工作，价值在于把 brain alignment 从静态相关现象推进到训练动态和能力分解。对 以及‘更像人脑’到底优化了什么。","technical_approach":"方法上这是一项跨 checkpoint 的系统分析。作者收集了 8 个模型尺寸、覆盖 300B tokens 的 34 个训练 checkpoint，比较 brain alignment 与两类语言能力的发展关系：formal competence 指语言规则和结构知识，functional competence 指世界知识和推理等更广义能力。这个设计的关键是引入时间轴和能力解耦，而不是只拿训练后单一模型做相关分析。若作者的观察成立，说明人脑语言网络更像是对形式语言处理高度敏感，而不是简单奖励“知道更多世界知识”的表征。","experiments":"摘要提供了较完整的实验设置：34 个 checkpoint、8 个模型大小、300B tokens 的训练跨度。核心结论有两条。第一，brain 的关系更弱，说明两者并不同步。摘要没有给出脑数据来源、相关指标、各模型尺寸范围和具体相关系数，因此无法判断这一结论在不同脑区或不同评测定义下是否稳定。最该看正文的是：这种分离在哪个训练阶段出现、是否存在尺寸阈值，以及 larger models 是否更早脱离 human language network 的对应关系。","authors_note":"作者包括 Antoine Prior work often reports model–brain similarity as a static result, but does not cleanly separate formal linguistic competence from broader functional competence such as world knowledge and reasoning.","importance":"This is an important mechanistic analysis for pretraining. The main lesson is that more training and better general capability do not automatically make a model more brain-like in the language network. The paper argues that brain alignment tracks formal linguistic competence more closely than functional competence, which sharpens how we should interpret scaling, token budgets, and brain-score trends over training.","technical_approach":"The approach is a training-dynamics study across checkpoints. The authors analyze 34 checkpoints spanning 300B tokens across 8 model sizes, relating brain alignment to the growth of two types of competence: formal linguistic competence, meaning knowledge of linguistic rules, and functional linguistic competence, meaning broader world knowledge and reasoning. The strength of the design is the time axis and the explicit decomposition of capability rather than a single end-state correlation.","experiments":"The abstract gives a fairly concrete setup: 34 checkpoints, 8 model sizes, and 300B training tokens. Martins","Marcely Zanon Boito"],"institutions":["Instituto de Telecomunicações","Unbabel","Instituto Superior Técnico"],"abstract":"We introduce Spire, a speech-augmented language model (LM) capable of both translating and transcribing speech input from English into 10 other languages as well as translating text input in both language directions. Medioni"],"institutions":["Amazon"],"abstract":"Large Language Models (LLMs) often suffer from mode collapse, repeatedly generating the same few completions even when many valid answers exist, limiting their diversity across a wide range of tasks. We introduce Group-Aware Policy Optimization (GAPO), a simple extension of the recent and popular Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) that computes rewards over the group as a whole. GAPO enables learning from the group-level properties such as diversity and coverage. We demonstrate GAPO using a frequency-aware reward function that encourages uniform sampling over valid LLM completions, and show that GAPO-trained models produce valid and more diverse model responses. Beyond this setup, GAPO generalizes to open-ended prompts and improves response diversity without compromising accuracy on standard LLM benchmarks (GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval, MMLU-Pro). Our code will be made publicly available.","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.12596","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/AnschelSBHGBBKAM25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.EMNLP-MAIN.1649"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/AnschelSBHGBBKAM25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-main.1649","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"GRPO 之类的 group-relative RL 在提升准确率的同时会让模型 mode collapse，只重复少数高分回答。问题是 reward 只在 per-sample 层面算，group 内部的多样性没有被当成信号。","importance":"RL 方法级的小而实的改进，对 pretrain↔posttrain 反哺有意义：多样性退化会伤害后续探索和 self-improvement。GAPO 把 reward 从 per-sample 扩展到 group-level，让 coverage/diversity 成为可优化量，这是一个很自然但此前被忽视的维度。","technical_approach":"GAPO = GRPO 的直接扩展：不再独立地给 group 里每个 sample 算 reward，而是把整个 group 作为单位计算 reward，从而可以编码 group 层面的属性（如频率分布的均匀性）。示例用了 frequency-aware reward，鼓励有效答案的均匀采样。关键区别在于优化目标变成了分布层面，而非单点最优。","experiments":"在有多个有效答案的任务上展示多样性提升，在开放式 prompt 上 diversity↑ 且 accuracy 不掉。具体数字和 baseline 设置看正文。","authors_note":"Amazon AGI（作者 Oron Anschel 等在 Amazon 视觉/多模态方向有工作）。"},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Group-relative RL (GRPO) improves accuracy but causes mode collapse because rewards are computed per sample; the diversity structure within a group is never a learning signal.","importance":"A small, practical RL tweak with real implications for pretrain↔posttrain loops: diversity collapse hurts exploration and self-improvement. Lifting reward computation to the group level makes coverage/diversity a first-class objective — a natural axis that prior RL-for-LLM work largely ignored.","technical_approach":"GAPO extends GRPO by computing reward over the whole group rather than independently per sample, enabling group-level properties (e.g., uniformity over valid completions) to enter the objective. The key shift is optimizing for a distributional target rather than per-sample optimum.","experiments":"On tasks with multiple valid answers, GAPO increases diversity; on open-ended prompts it improves diversity without sacrificing accuracy. Carvalho","Yingji Zhang","Harriet Unsworth","André Freitas"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/CarvalhoZUF25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.EMNLP-DEMOS.57"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/CarvalhoZUF25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-demos.57","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：语言 VAE 一直被自回归解码器的 posterior collapse（潜变量被忽略）和缺少可解释潜空间探针这两个问题卡住，导致它长期被当成“做得出来但不好用”的语言建模分支。作者试图同时把“能训练起来”和“能分析 latent 学到了什么”这两件事补齐，因此不只是提一个 VAE 结构，也把 LangSpace 作为系统化 probing 框架一起提出。","importance":"它的价值更偏方法级加分析工具级，而不是直接改写主流 pretrain 范式。对 pretrain 研究的启示在于：如果语言 latent variable model 想重新变得有竞争力，关键不只是提升生成指标，而是证明潜变量确实承载了可控、可探测、不同于 next-token hidden state 的信息；这篇工作如果成立，至少给“语言 VAE 是否还能做”提供了更清晰的实验入口。","technical_approach":"核心方法是构建一个面向语言模型的 VAE 训练与分析框架，而不是单点修补某个 KL annealing 技巧。LangVAE 这一侧大概率围绕稳定 latent 使用率做设计，比如在编码器、潜变量注入位置、KL 调度或解码约束上避免 decoder 完全绕开 z；LangSpace 这一侧则把潜空间 probing 标准化，用成对控制实验去测语义、句法或属性可分离性。和早期语言 VAE 工作相比，它的区别不在“也用了 VAE”，而在把训练可行性和潜空间可验证性捆在一起评估，避免只报 ELBO 却不知道 latent 有没有用。","experiments":"从给定信息看，实验应当主要回答两件事：第一，LangVAE 是否比常见语言 VAE 基线更少 collapse、latent 利用率更高；第二，LangSpace 是否能揭示潜变量编码了哪些语言属性。当前没有摘要，因此无法可靠写出模型参数、数据规模、具体 baseline、指标和提升数字。能合理预期的有效实验包括：重建/生成质量与 KL 使用率的联合报告，和对潜空间线性 probe、插值、属性控制的一组对照；若论文做得扎实，还应展示更强 decoder 下 latent 仍不完全失效这一点。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a long-standing failure mode of language VAEs: strong autoregressive decoders ignore the latent code, and the field lacks a clean way to verify what the latent space actually captures. The point is not just to make a VAE train, but to make its latent variables empirically inspectable and therefore worth using.","importance":"This is primarily a method-plus-analysis contribution rather than a paradigm shift for mainstream pretraining. Its relevance to pretraining is that latent-variable language modeling will only matter again if it can show non-trivial information is carried in z, beyond what next-token hidden states already provide; this work appears to move the discussion from “can we optimize ELBO?” to “does the latent space encode anything useful and controllable?”.","technical_approach":"The core idea is a joint framework for building and probing language-model VAEs, not a single training trick. On the LangVAE side, the likely design focus is to preserve latent usage under a strong decoder through architectural placement and training controls; on the LangSpace side, the paper appears to systematize probing so semantic or syntactic factors in the latent space can be measured rather than assumed. The key difference from earlier language VAE papers is the coupling of optimization viability with latent-space validation.","experiments":"Given the missing abstract, the experiments can only be summarized at a high level. Provides an aggressive yet practical recipe for ultra-low-bit quantization, highly valuable for deploying massive models on edge devices or VRAM-constrained environments.","technical_approach":"Proposes the RCP (Rotate, Clip, Partition) quantization scheme. It first applies orthogonal rotation to disperse outliers in activations and weights. Then, it uses clipping to handle residual extreme values. Finally, it employs a learnable non-uniform quantizer (Partition) to optimally fit the bell-shaped distribution of the rotated weights, maximizing the expressiveness of 2-bit representations.","experiments":"Validated on LLaMA models. Under the extreme W2A4KV4 setting, RCP significantly outperforms existing uniform quantization or rotation-only methods (like QuIP), keeping perplexity degradation within a usable range.","authors_note":""},"tags":["quantization","rotation","kv-cache","w2a4"]},{"id":"ChristopoulouCLBW25","title":"SparsePO: Controlling Preference Alignment of LLMs via Sparse Token Masks","authors":["Fenia Christopoulou","Ronald Cardenas","Gerasimos Lampouras","Haitham Bou-Ammar","Jun Wang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/ChristopoulouCLBW25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/ChristopoulouCLBW25","_ee":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-emnlp.1389/","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"标准的偏好优化（如 DPO）对回复中的所有 token 均匀计算损失，但实际上决定人类偏好的往往只是少数几个关键 token（如某个事实错误或冒犯性词汇），全局计算会导致模型过度拟合噪声并遗忘通用能力。","importance":"方法级贡献。改进了 Post-training 的核心 Loss 设计，提升了对齐信号的信噪比，有效缓解了对齐税（Alignment Tax）。","technical_approach":"提出 SparsePO（稀疏偏好优化）。通过启发式差异对比或外部模型评估，识别出 chosen 和 rejected 回复中真正导致偏好差异的关键 token，并生成稀疏掩码（Sparse Token Masks）。在计算 DPO 损失时，仅对这些被 mask 选中的关键 token 施加梯度更新，忽略无关的通用表述。","experiments":"在标准对齐基准（如 AlpacaEval, MT-Bench）上测试。相比密集型 DPO，SparsePO 不仅取得了更高的偏好胜率，还显著降低了基础模型在推理和知识问答任务上的能力退化（灾难性遗忘）。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"Standard preference optimization (like DPO) computes loss uniformly across all tokens in a response, even though human preference is usually determined by a few critical tokens (e.g., a factual error or offensive word). Global computation leads to noise overfitting and capability forgetting.","importance":"Methodological contribution. Improves the core loss design in post-training, enhancing the signal-to-noise ratio of alignment and effectively mitigating the alignment tax.","technical_approach":"Introduces SparsePO (Sparse Preference Optimization). It identifies the critical tokens that actually cause the preference difference between chosen and rejected responses (via heuristic diffs or external evaluators) and generates sparse token masks. During DPO loss computation, gradients are only applied to these masked critical tokens, ignoring irrelevant general phrasing.","experiments":"Tested on alignment benchmarks (AlpacaEval, MT-Bench). Compared to dense DPO, SparsePO achieves higher preference win rates and significantly reduces capability degradation (catastrophic forgetting) on reasoning and knowledge tasks.","authors_note":""},"tags":["preference-optimization","sparse-token","alignment"]},{"id":"DaiZMP25","title":"Improving Influence-based Instruction Tuning Data Selection for Balanced Learning of Diverse Capabilities","authors":["Qirun Dai","Dylan Zhang","Jiaqi W. Ma","Hao Peng"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/DaiZMP25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/DaiZMP25","_ee":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-emnlp.373/","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"must_read","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作关注 instruction tuning 数据选择中的一个老问题：按 influence 选数据虽然能提升平均性能，但常常把模型推向少数强势能力，导致能力分布失衡。过去很多方法默认“高影响样本越多越好”，对多能力场景中的覆盖和冲突处理不够细；这篇论文显然是在修这个缺口。","importance":"它是典型的方法级工作，但对 posttrain→pretrain 接口很有价值。数据选择不仅影响最终平均分，还决定模型学到的是哪类能力和能力间的配比；如果 influence-based 选择可以做得更平衡，它对高质量 SFT 数据构造、混合数据配比、连续训练中的 curriculum 设计都有直接参考意义。","technical_approach":"从标题看，作者不是否定 influence-based selection，而是在其上做改进，使 diverse capabilities 的学习更均衡。关键点应在于：把“样本对整体性能的影响”改写成“样本对不同能力维度的影响”，再加一个平衡或校准机制，避免高影响但单一能力的数据垄断预算；这类设计有效的前提是能力划分足够可测，否则平衡目标容易退化成启发式重采样。","experiments":"未提供摘要，因此无法确认任务覆盖、模型规模和具体提升。一个可信的实验框架通常需要：多维能力基准、与随机采样/长度采样/原始 influence 采样对比、相同 token budget 下的平均分与最差能力分数，并最好报告 trade-off——例如平均分略升但长尾能力显著改善，或在相同均值下方差更低。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a common failure mode in instruction-tuning data selection: influence-based sampling may improve average quality but often overfits the tuning budget to a few dominant capabilities. Earlier work usually treats influence as a single scalar objective, which is too coarse when the goal is balanced capability development.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution with practical significance for the pretrain-to-posttrain interface. Data selection is not only about maximizing aggregate score; it shapes which capabilities get learned and which are starved. A better-balanced influence selector would matter for SFT curation, mixture design, and curriculum choices in continual training.","technical_approach":"The title suggests an improved influence-based selection method rather than a replacement. The likely idea is to decompose or calibrate sample influence with respect to multiple capability groups, then allocate data in a way that avoids spending the entire budget on a narrow slice of skills; this is appealing because it preserves the data-efficiency logic of influence methods while correcting their imbalance, though it depends on having a meaningful capability taxonomy.","experiments":"No abstract is available, so model scale, datasets, and exact gains cannot be confirmed. ordinary factual error: factual reliability may look strong at high confidence and then collapse under perturbation or distribution shift. For pretraining, that matters because next-token likelihood and post-training preference optimization may produce confident but brittle factual representations, which would affect how we think about curriculum, cooldown, knowledge refresh, and uncertainty estimation.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is an analysis framework that links confidence, correctness, and robustness degradation. The key idea is not another generic calibration metric, but identifying a regime where confidence remains high while factual accuracy drops sharply under paraphrase, evidence conflict, retrieval distraction, or temporal shift. A reliable early-warning signal would reduce wasted runs and make it easier to compare optimizers, schedules, data mixtures, precision settings, and parallelization strategies on something more informative than post-mortem loss curves.","technical_approach":"The main contribution is likely a reliability metric designed to predict failure risk before obvious divergence appears. Unlike raw loss or gradient norm thresholds, such a metric probably aggregates temporal instability, layer imbalance, gradient–parameter mismatch, or train–validation drift into a forward-looking signal. Lee"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/JungYKL25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.EMNLP-MAIN.409"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/JungYKL25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-main.409","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：知识蒸馏里按序列整体对齐分布太粗，无法稳定地把教师模型的 token 级行为传给学生。传统 distillation 往往只在句级 logits 或最终目标上做匹配，结果是学生在难 token、长尾 token 和不确定位置上学得不均衡；作者提出 token-wise 的细粒度散度控制来修正这个问题。","importance":"它的重要性更偏方法级和训练信号设计，而不是范式级改变。对 pretrain/post-train 接口都有启发：如果 token 级蒸馏信号能更稳定地分配学习强度，就能改善小模型压缩、teacher-student continual training，甚至影响如何设计 AR LM 的辅助 loss；但从标题看，它更像对现有蒸馏框架的精修，而不是全新蒸馏范式。","technical_approach":"核心方法应是：在每个 token 位置上显式控制学生与教师之间的分布散度，而不是统一使用单一温度或全局 KL。这样做的原因很直接——不同 token 的预测熵和教师置信度差异很大，统一蒸馏强度会让容易位置被过拟合、困难位置又学不到位。所谓 fine-grained divergence control，大概率涉及对 token 级 KL/JS/反向 KL 的重加权、裁剪或自适应调节。它与标准 KD 的关键区别，不在于“是否蒸馏”，而在于“蒸馏信号如何按位置分配”。摘要缺失，因此无法确认控制变量是教师熵、学生误差、token 频率还是 curriculum 机制。","experiments":"没有摘要就不能给出具体提升数字。可以合理预期的实验会覆盖：不同规模 teacher/student 组合；语言建模或指令跟随任务上的 perplexity / accuracy / downstream 指标；与 standard KD、hard-label CE、temperature tuning、sequence-level distillation 的比较；以及 token 频率或置信度分桶分析。若论文做得扎实，最有价值的证据会是：收益主要集中在高熵 token、长尾 token 或长上下文位置，而不是平均指标上的微弱涨点，但目前无法确认。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a familiar weakness in distillation: sequence-level matching is too coarse to reliably transfer token-level behavior from a teacher to a student. Standard KD often applies one global divergence objective, which tends to overfit easy positions and under-train hard or uncertain tokens; this work proposes token-wise divergence control to fix that allocation problem.","importance":"This is mainly a method and training-signal paper rather than a paradigm shift. It matters for both pretraining and post-training because better token-level distillation can improve small-model compression, teacher-student continuation, and the design of auxiliary LM losses. From the title, though, it looks like a careful refinement of KD rather than a replacement of existing distillation frameworks.","technical_approach":"The likely idea is to regulate teacher-student divergence at each token position instead of using one uniform KD setting. The rationale is straightforward: token difficulty varies a lot, and a single global KL or temperature mismatches how much supervision each position needs. “Fine-grained divergence control” probably means adaptive reweighting, clipping, or scheduling of token-level KL/JS-type losses based on teacher confidence, student error, or token statistics. The key difference from standard KD is not whether to distill, but how to allocate distillation strength across positions.","experiments":"Without the abstract, specific numbers would be speculative. The most informative result, if present, would be gains concentrated on high-entropy, long-tail, or long-context tokens rather than tiny average improvements, but this cannot be confirmed here.","authors_note":""},"tags":["distillation","training-objective","token-level","optimization"]},{"id":"JungZC25","title":"Code Execution as Grounded Supervision for LLM 的推理监督大多来自最终答案或人工过程标注，缺少可验证、低噪声的中间监督。尤其在数学和程序化推理里，仅靠 outcome supervision 容易让模型学会表面格式而不是正确过程；作者尝试把代码执行结果当作 grounded supervision 来约束推理。","importance":"这项工作对 pretrain 本身不是直接改写，但对 pretrain ↔ post-train 接口很有启发。它提供的是训练信号层面的改进：把不可验证的自然语言推理链，部分转化为可执行、可判定的监督。这类信号如果稳定，能影响合成推理数据、self-improvement 和 tool-augmented reasoning 的训练设计。贡献量级取决于它是否证明执行监督能系统性优于仅看最终答案。","technical_approach":"核心方法大概率是：让模型把推理步骤映射到可执行代码，再通过执行结果给过程或答案提供反馈。这样设计有效的原因在于，执行器提供的是外部语义约束，而不是语言模型内部自洽；代码一旦可运行，就能把“听起来对”与“算出来对”分开。与普通 chain-of-thought 监督相比，它更强调 grounded verification。代价是适用范围受限：只有能被代码表达和执行验证的任务才受益，而且代码生成错误、执行环境限制和测试覆盖不足都会带来新噪声。缺少摘要，无法确认作者做的是训练时蒸馏、RL、拒答过滤，还是数据构造。","experiments":"具体实验事实无法从当前信息确认。按题目判断，关键评测应包括数学/符号/表格/程序推理任务，与 answer-only supervision、CoT supervision、self-consistency 或 tool-augmented baseline 对比；最好还应区分“答案正确但过程错”和“过程可执行且答案对”的比例。如果论文质量高，最有价值的 ablation 会是：只看执行通过率是否足够、代码中间表示是否优于自由文本 CoT、以及执行监督在样本效率上的改善，但这些都不能在无摘要条件下写成确定结果。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a key weakness in reasoning supervision: most LLM training relies on final answers or manually written rationales, both of which are noisy and weakly grounded. In math and procedural reasoning, outcome-only supervision often teaches format imitation rather than valid reasoning; this work uses code execution as a grounded supervision signal.","importance":"This is not a direct pretraining paper, but it is highly relevant to the pretrain–post-train interface. The contribution is about better training signals: converting parts of natural-language reasoning into executable, verifiable supervision. If robust, that has implications for synthetic reasoning data, self-improvement pipelines, and tool-grounded training. The significance depends on whether execution-based supervision consistently beats answer-only supervision rather than helping only in narrow tasks.","technical_approach":"The likely method is to map reasoning steps into executable code and use execution outcomes to supervise either the process, the answer, or both. The design is attractive because an executor supplies an external semantic check instead of relying on model self-consistency. Once a solution can be executed, it becomes easier to separate plausible text from actually correct reasoning. Compared with standard CoT supervision, the key difference is grounded verification. The trade-off is scope: only tasks expressible in code benefit, and code generation errors, environment constraints, and limited test coverage can introduce new failure modes.","experiments":"Specific empirical results cannot be stated from the provided metadata. Not Foes: Investigating Vocabulary Overlap in Multilingual Language Models","authors":["Julie Kallini","Dan Jurafsky","Christopher Potts","Martijn Bartelds"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/KalliniJPB25","doi":""},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/KalliniJPB25","_ee":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-emnlp.1153/","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：多语种模型里的词表重叠，尤其是“假朋友词”（拼写相近但语义不同），到底是在帮模型共享知识，还是在制造跨语言干扰。过去关于共享词表的讨论常把 overlap 视为天然有利，或简单担心 negative transfer；作者的结论从标题就很明确：false friends 并不一定是坏事。","importance":"这对 multilingual pretraining 很重要，因为它直接碰到 tokenizer 和共享表示的核心设计。若结论成立，当前对“跨语言同形异义会伤害模型”的直觉需要被修正；这意味着构建多语词表时，不应机械回避字符串重叠，而应更细致地看共享子词是否仍带来表示和优化上的收益。这是数据表示层面的结论，影响真实系统设计。","technical_approach":"方法上大概率是围绕 vocabulary overlap 做系统对比：区分 cognates、true overlap、false friends 和无重叠词，观察它们对多语模型表示和任务性能的影响。这个问题必须在控制语言亲缘、频率和脚本相似度的前提下分析，否则 overlap 的好处很容易和语料共享、频次优势混在一起。标题里的 are not foes 暗示作者发现，即便共享 token 表面上引入语义冲突，模型也可能通过上下文把不同语言中的含义分开，从而保留词表共享带来的统计效率。","experiments":"没有摘要，无法给出具体语种、模型和提升幅度。高质量实验通常会包括：不同 tokenizer 设计下的多语预训练或微调表现；跨语言检索/迁移任务；对 false friends 子集的专项评测；以及表示空间分析，看模型是否把同形异义词按语言上下文分开。如果论文核心论点扎实，最有价值的结果会是：去掉这类 overlap 并没有带来预期收益，甚至损伤低资源语言迁移，但目前不能写成定量结论。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper examines whether vocabulary overlap in multilingual language models—especially false friends, where form overlaps but meaning diverges—helps transfer or causes harmful interference. Prior discussions often assume shared vocabulary is beneficial by default, or warn vaguely about negative transfer; the title signals a more nuanced conclusion: false friends are not necessarily harmful.","importance":"This matters directly for multilingual pretraining because it touches the tokenizer and shared-representation design choice at the core of the stack. If the claim holds, the common intuition that homographic cross-lingual overlap should be avoided is too crude. That would mean multilingual vocabulary design should not aggressively eliminate overlap, since contextual disambiguation may preserve the efficiency benefits of sharing subwords.","technical_approach":"The likely method is a controlled study of vocabulary overlap, separating true cognates, false friends, and non-overlapping items, then measuring their effect on representation and task transfer. This has to control for language relatedness, script similarity, and token frequency, otherwise overlap benefits can be confounded with data overlap. It changes how we interpret model capability, safety risk, and the reliability of benchmark results. If the paper shows that leakage impacts reproduction and detectability differently, that would matter for deduplication policy, data audits, synthetic data filtering, and contamination-aware evaluation. The contribution sits at the intersection of data governance and measurement.","technical_approach":"The likely approach is to inject or identify controlled leakage in pretraining data and then measure two things separately: the model’s tendency to reproduce leaked content and the effectiveness of leakage detection methods. A model may be able to reason, yet still fail when the needed facts are missing from its parameters, outdated, or spread across multiple evidence hops.","importance":"Its importance lies in treating search as part of the reasoning agent rather than as a simple retrieval prepend. For the pretrain–posttrain loop, the broader lesson is that stronger reasoning does not have to come only from storing more knowledge in weights; it can also come from a better act-observe-reason interface.","technical_approach":"From the title, Search-o1 is likely an agentic search-enhanced reasoning framework in which the model decides when to search, what to search for, and how to integrate the results into an evolving reasoning trace. The difference from standard RAG is usually that search is multi-step and conditioned on intermediate reasoning state rather than being a single retrieval step before answer generation. This is a sensible design because information needs often emerge gradually in hard tasks. Standard preference learning often treats pairwise preferences as clean supervision, but real preference data is noisy, shifted, and annotator-dependent, which makes reward models unstable and easy to exploit.","importance":"The contribution is primarily about posttraining signal quality, with indirect relevance to pretraining. A more robust reward model raises the ceiling of RLHF or RLAIF and helps separate genuine behavior improvement from mere reward gaming. If the optimal-transport component yields consistent gains, this is a useful method-level advance.","technical_approach":"From the title, APLOT combines adaptive preference learning with optimal transport, likely replacing rigid instance-wise preference fitting with a softer distribution-matching view. This is plausible because OT is well suited to aligning noisy or heterogeneous preference distributions and can reduce damage from mislabeled or atypical pairs. The trade-off is complexity: more compute, more tuning, and less simplicity than standard Bradley-Terry or pairwise ranking objectives. The distinguishing idea is probably not just reweighting examples, but modeling geometry between preference distributions.","experiments":"Without the abstract, there is no basis for naming datasets or reporting gains. It mathematically solves the unbiased gradient problem in ultra-low precision training, serving as a key enabler for on-device continual pretraining and fine-tuning.","technical_approach":"Introduces Stochastic Rounding (SR) for low-bit training. When quantizing weight updates and activations, instead of deterministic nearest-neighbor rounding, it uses probabilistic rounding based on the numerical distance to quantization nodes. This mechanism ensures that the mathematical expectation of the quantized gradient equals the full-precision gradient, thereby maintaining the correct optimization direction at extremely low bit-widths.","experiments":"Conducted LLM fine-tuning experiments on resource-constrained hardware. Demonstrated that low-bit (e.g., 4-bit/8-bit) training with stochastic rounding achieves convergence curves and final perplexities nearly identical to FP16, fully unlocking training capabilities on edge devices."},"tags":["low-bit","stochastic-rounding","training"]},{"id":"LiuCHHWKRYS25","title":"On Relation-Specific Neurons in Large Language Models","authors":["Yihong Liu","Runsheng Chen","Lea Hirlimann","Ahmad Dawar Hakimi","Mingyang Wang","Amir Hossein Kargaran","Sascha Rothe","François Yvon","Hinrich Schütze"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"EMNLP","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/emnlp/LiuCHHWKRYS25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.EMNLP-MAIN.52"},"_dblp_key":"conf/emnlp/LiuCHHWKRYS25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.emnlp-main.52","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":7,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文研究的核心问题是：LLM 里是否存在与特定关系类型稳定对应的神经元，以及这些神经元到底是可解释机制还是只是相关性伪影。过去神经元解释工作常停留在词性、主题或单一事实激活层面，但“关系”是更接近知识组织单元的粒度。","importance":"它的价值主要在机制分析与可控编辑接口上。若关系特异神经元确实存在且可干预，这会影响我们对知识存储位置、模型编辑粒度、蒸馏/剪枝风险以及 reward 或 verifier 模块可解释性的理解；对 pretrain 来说，这是在问表征是否按关系结构被局部化。","technical_approach":"方法上大概率是先定义一组关系类型，再通过激活统计、因果干预或神经元打分去定位与这些关系强相关的神经元。与早期只做可视化或 probing 不同，这类工作通常会加入 ablation、激活替换、重加权或知识编辑实验，去验证这些神经元不是仅仅响应表面模板，而是对关系回忆有功能性作用。","experiments":"实验应围绕“定位是否稳定、跨模板是否泛化、干预是否影响特定关系而不过度伤及其他能力”来展开。给定信息没有摘要与结果表，因此无法确认模型族、层分布、定位比例、准确率变化或编辑成功率；如果论文完整，它最关键的证据应是 causal ablation 和 cross-template robustness，而不是只看相关性热图。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks whether large language models contain neurons that are specific to relation types, and whether those neurons are functionally meaningful rather than just correlated artifacts. Prior neuron-level interpretability often focused on lexical, topical, or fact-specific activation; relation-level structure is a more useful unit for understanding stored knowledge.","importance":"Its importance is mainly in mechanistic interpretability and controllable intervention. If relation-specific neurons can be reliably identified and causally manipulated, that would inform how knowledge is organized in pretrained models and how safely we can edit, distill, or prune them without damaging unrelated capabilities.","technical_approach":"The likely method is to define a set of relation categories, score neurons by relation sensitivity, and then validate them with interventions. The key design difference from pure probing is whether the paper performs causal tests such as neuron ablation, activation steering, or cross-template transfer, which is what separates genuine relation encoding from prompt-template artifacts.","experiments":"The decisive experiments should test stability across prompts, layers, and model instances, plus selective impact under intervention. 收益。若论文做得扎实，还会报告路由熵、专家负载不均衡、token dropping 率等诊断指标。没有摘要，无法确认覆盖的模型规模和具体 tricks 列表。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a gap in sparse MoE research: there is no widely For pretraining, its value is substantial because MoE design choices interact strongly: routing, expert count, capacity factor, auxiliary balancing losses, and system configuration cannot be judged from loss curves or average throughput alone.
